acceptance_criteria: The model contains an explicit maturity or rollover mechanism.
It can produce at least one case where similar debt stocks produce different outcomes because refinancing conditions differ.
Outputs are simple enough to explain in the blog without pretending to model the whole securities market.
Tests document what the surface illuminates and what it abstracts away.
constraints: Prefer the smallest model that makes rollover/refinancing intelligible.
Do not build a full yield-curve, dealer, or auction microstructure model.
If the result lands weaker than expected, document that honestly.
description: Extend or companion one of the existing sovereign/user models with a minimal maturity ladder, rollover share, refinancing rate, and optionally a small holder split. The purpose is to distinguish debt stock from refinancing pressure and to show why the same debt ratio can behave differently depending on maturity and market-access conditions.
goal: Create a minimal computational surface that shows how maturity structure, refinancing pressure, and securities dynamics can change fiscal adjustment without requiring a full bond-market model.
implementation_notes: Implemented in repo by Claude. Added sovereign_securities.py with short/long debt, rollover pressure, surplus-funded paydowns, issuer/user regime differences, and the rollover_comparison.png figure. README and experiment entrypoints were updated accordingly.
Planner verification on 2026-04-25: targeted sovereign_securities and external_constraint test modules passed cleanly.
title: Toy sovereign-securities and rollover extension
